title: ceropegia pruinosior
intro: Ceropegia pruinosior, also known as the woolly-stemmed milk-pair or ceropegia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to southern Africa and is known for its unique, woolly stems and white, funnel-shaped flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The leaves and stems of the ceropegia pruinosior can be used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, to harness its health benefits.
